summary:
KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This class represents an event where a map task attempt has finished.

CORE LOGIC:

The core logic of this class is to record the successful completion of a map task attempt.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The inputs to this class are the task attempt ID, task type, task status, map finish time, finish time, hostname, port, rack name, state, counters, and splits. The output is a data structure that represents the successful completion of the map task attempt.

DEPENDENCIES:

This class depends on the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.jobhistory package and other classes in the same package.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This chunk of code is a class named ReduceAttemptFinishedEvent. It is used to record the successful completion of a reduce attempt.

CORE LOGIC:
The class has a constructor that takes in various parameters such as the task attempt ID, task type, task status, shuffle finish time, sort finish time, finish time, hostname, port, rack name, state, counters, and an array of splits. It also has methods to get the task ID, attempt ID, task type, task status, finish time of the sort phase, finish time of the shuffle phase, finish time of the attempt, hostname, port, rack name, state, counters, and splits.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The class takes in input parameters such as task attempt ID, task type, task status, shuffle finish time, sort finish time, finish time, hostname, port, rack name, state, counters, and an array of splits. It also outputs the task ID, attempt ID, task type, task status, finish time of the sort phase, finish time of the shuffle phase, finish time of the attempt, hostname, port, rack name, state, counters, and splits.

DEPENDENCIES:
The class depends on the following classes: TaskAttemptID, TaskID, TaskType, Counters, ProgressSplitsBlock, SystemClock, TimelineEvent, TimelineMetric, and various utility classes for Avro and timeline metrics.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This code represents an event to record successful task completion.

CORE LOGIC:
The code sets the task ID, attempt ID, task type, task status, finish time, rack name, hostname, state, and counters for the task attempt.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The code takes inputs such as the task attempt ID, task type, task status, finish time, rack name, hostname, state, and counters, and outputs a data object representing the task attempt finished event.

DEPENDENCIES:
The code depends on the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.jobhistory package and other classes and interfaces within that package. It also depends on the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.Counters class for handling task attempt counters.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This class represents an event where a task attempt has failed or been killed.

CORE LOGIC:

The core logic of this class is to record the unsuccessful completion of task attempts. It includes the task ID, task type, attempt ID, finish time, hostname, port, rack name, error, status, and counters.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The inputs to this class are the task ID, task type, status, finish time, hostname, port, rack name, error, counters, and splits. The outputs are the task ID, task type, attempt ID, finish time, hostname, port, rack name, error, status, and counters.

DEPENDENCIES:

This class depends on the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.jobhistory package and the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.util.JobHistoryEventUtils class.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This chunk of code is responsible for recording the failure of a task.

CORE LOGIC:

The code uses the provided data to create a TaskFailedEvent object, which includes the task ID, finish time, task type, error, status, failed attempt ID, and counters.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The code takes in the necessary inputs to create a TaskFailedEvent object, including the task ID, finish time, task type, error, status, failed attempt ID, and counters. It then sets these inputs as the attributes of the TaskFailedEvent object.

DEPENDENCIES:

The code depends on the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.jobhistory package and its dependencies.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This chunk of code is responsible for recording the successful completion of a task. It includes the necessary attributes and methods to create and store the event data, as well as retrieve the relevant information.

CORE LOGIC:

The core logic of this code involves creating a TaskFinishedEvent object with the required parameters, such as task ID, successful task attempt ID, finish time, task type, status, and counters. The object is then serialized into an Avro data structure for storage or transmission.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The inputs to this code include the task ID, successful task attempt ID, finish time, task type, status, and counters. The outputs include the serialized TaskFinishedEvent object, which can be stored or transmitted.

DEPENDENCIES:

This code depends on the Apache Hadoop MapReduce framework and its associated classes and interfaces, such as TaskID, TaskAttemptID, TaskType, Counters, and HistoryEvent. It also depends on external libraries such as Avro and TimelineEvent.
